item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it enough for a +1. Would've really liked to see that log, where the submersible distracts SCP 3924, end a different way. If the Foundations' interference actually caused some aspect of the entity to reach a recognizable height, or made the SCP dive without transmission, or something else interesting that would've been cool. As it stands, the Foundation intervenes but just gets smacked by the SCP, and nothing really changes. If the damage the SCP ends up taking was as a result of Foundation interference, that would be really compelling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Intriguing. I like how it has its own deal going on but isn't too busy to attack invaders. Good sense of mystery here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought that image looked suspiciously like a destroyer of some variety. I like what you've got here, but I definitely want to see… more… I guess. More of an explanation or maybe some kind of communication from this thing? I dunno. That's a symptom of a good SCP in my book. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Image is an edited version of the 'sonar image of the shipwreck of the Latvian Navy ship Virsaitis', released under Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC BY-SA 4.0). Original image, with licensing and further attribution, is available here.
